revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Izumianashi-jinja]][[ja:泉穴師神社]] {{Expand Japanese|泉穴師神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Izumianashi Shrine | native_name = 泉穴師神社 | image = Izumianashi-jinja, haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Izumianashi Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.496726|135.419834}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|lt=Ame no Oshihomimi|de|Amenooshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|fa|آمه-نو-اوشیهومیمی|fr|Ame-no-oshihomimi|it|Ame-no-oshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|ko|아메노오시호미미|mni|ꯑꯃꯦ-ꯅꯣ-ꯑꯣꯁꯤꯍꯣꯃꯤꯃꯤ|ru|Амэноосихомими но Микото|th|อาเมะ โนะ โอชิโฮมิมิ|uk|Аменоосіхомімі но Мікото|zh|天忍穗耳尊|qid=Q462555|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} }} {{nihongo|'''Izumianashi Shrine'''|泉穴師神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Ni-no-Miya|lt=Ni-no-Miya|qid=Q134917533|1=Ni-no-Miya}} ** P1001: {{ill|Izumi Province|lt=Izumi Province|ca|Província d'Izumi|ckb|پارێزگای ئیزومی|cs|Provincie Izumi|de|Provinz Izumi|en|Izumi Province|es|Provincia de Izumi|fa|ولایت ایزومی|fr|Province d'Izumi|gl|Provincia de Izumi|hak|Izumi-koet|id|Provinsi Izumi|it|Izumi (provincia)|ja|和泉国|ko|이즈미국|lt|Idzumio žemė|nl|Izumi (provincie)|pt|Província de Izumi|ru|Идзуми (провинция)|simple|Izumi Province|sk|Izumi (provincia)|sr|Провинција Изуми|tr|Izumi (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Ідзумі|zh_min_nan|Izumi-kok|zh_yue|和泉國|zh|和泉國|qid=Q1047117|1=Izumi Province}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kanpei-sha|lt=Kanpei-sha|simple|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11417778|ltq=Q11417778|qid=Q11417778|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 27 ** P155: {{ill|Q11431309|ltq=Q11431309|qid=Q11431309|1=Yagi Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Hyōzu Shrine|lt=Hyōzu Shrine|ja|兵主神社 (岸和田市)|qid=Q85593661|1=Hyōzu Shrine (Kishiwada)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q873553|ltq=Q873553|qid=Q873553|1=Izumiōtsu}} * {{ill|Q1047117|ltq=Q1047117|qid=Q1047117|1=Izumi Province}} * {{ill|Izumi district|lt=Izumi district|ja|和泉郡|zh|和泉郡|qid=Q10982247|1=Izumi district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.496726|135.419834}} == image (P18) == * Izumianashi-jinja, haiden.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|lt=Ame no Oshihomimi|de|Amenooshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|fa|آمه-نو-اوشیهومیمی|fr|Ame-no-oshihomimi|it|Ame-no-oshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|ko|아메노오시호미미|mni|ꯑꯃꯦ-ꯅꯣ-ꯑꯣꯁꯤꯍꯣꯃꯤꯃꯤ|ru|Амэноосихомими но Микото|th|อาเมะ โนะ โอชิโฮมิมิ|uk|Аменоосіхомімі но Мікото|zh|天忍穗耳尊|qid=Q462555|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} * {{ill|Q11110848|ltq=Q11110848|qid=Q11110848|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20041124014737/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=40201 == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 180994 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917326|ltq=Q134917326|qid=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Unranked|lt=Unranked|ja|无位|qid=Q11504610|1=Unranked}} * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} * {{ill|Q11123280|ltq=Q11123280|qid=Q11123280|1=Senior Fifth Rank}} * {{ill|Q11071127|ltq=Q11071127|qid=Q11071127|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 255881132 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2120105006903 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 00639482 == Commons category (P373) == * Izumianashi-jinja == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Q135018062|ltq=Q135018062|qid=Q135018062|1=Engishiki seat}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府泉大津市豊中町1丁目1-1', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Izumianashi-jinja}} [[JA:泉穴師神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11554908}} == Japanese content == {{?? |?? = Izumianashi Shrine |?? = [[File:Izumianashi-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |??? = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumiotsu City|Q873553|qid=Q873553|1=Izumiōtsu}} Toyonaka-cho 1-chome 1-1 |?? = {{?????2????|34|29|47.5|N|135|25|11.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Izumianashi Shrine}} |?? = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|qid=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}
{{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|qid=Q11110848|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} |?? = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (two minor shrines)
{{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q1047117|qid=Q1047117|1=Izumi Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#Various shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=fusha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |?? = First year of {{ill|lt=Hakuh?|Q11580612|qid=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} ({{ill|lt=672|Q30601|qid=Q30601|1=672}}), with variant traditions |?? = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}
front-facing chidori hafu gable |?? = |??? = |?? = October 10 |?? = |?? = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Izumianashi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Izumianashi Shrine''' (??????????) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumiotsu City|Q873553|qid=Q873553|1=Izumiōtsu}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, the {{ill|lt=Ninomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q1047117|qid=Q1047117|1=Izumi Province}}, and its former status in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Fu-sha|qid=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}}. "The Forest of Izumianashi Shrine" is selected as one of the {{ill|lt=Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|Q11439936|qid=Q11439936|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen}}. === Enshrined deities === In the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaicho|Q11590704|qid=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} compiled in {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|qid=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12), Tenfukishin and Sakomazuchi-no-Okami are listed as this shrine's deities. However, in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Jinja Meisaicho|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=1894|Q7843|qid=Q7843|1=1894}} (Meiji 27), the deities are {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|qid=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} and {{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|qid=Q11110848|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}}, and this shrine follows that interpretation. Izumiotsu City developed a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=textile industry|1=UNKNOWN}} in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}} and remains a major textile-producing area today; Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto is known as a deity associated with {{ill|lt=weaving|Q28823|qid=Q28823|1=Textile}}. * Main deities - {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|qid=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}, {{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|qid=Q11110848|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} === History === According to shrine tradition, this shrine was founded in the first year of {{ill|lt=Hakuh?|Q11580612|qid=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} ({{ill|lt=672|Q30601|qid=Q30601|1=672}}), though other traditions place its founding in the era of {{ill|lt=Empress Jing?|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} or at the time of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu's eastern expedition|Q11590444|qid=Q11590444|1=Jimmu's Eastern Expedition}}, so the details are uncertain. According to the Izumi provincial tax register of {{ill|lt=Tenpy?|Q1199879|qid=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 10 ({{ill|lt=738|Q23585|qid=Q23585|1=738}}) in the {{ill|lt=Sh?s?in documents|Q11545362|qid=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Sh?mu|Q349263|qid=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}} granted it 1,300 koku of shrine lands together with {{ill|lt=?tori Taisha|Q705151|qid=Q705151|1=Ōtori taisha}}. The {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} states that in {{ill|lt=J?gan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=J?gan|1=Jōgan}} 7 ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|qid=Q30350|1=865}}) it was granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, while the {{ill|lt=Ruiju Kokushi|Q1038639|qid=Q1038639|1=Ruijū Kokushi}} states that in J?gan 10 ({{ill|lt=868|Q25061|qid=Q25061|1=868}}) it was granted Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Genk?|Q1194556|qid=Q1194556|1=Genkō (1331–1334)}} ({{ill|lt=1331|Q6000|qid=Q6000|1=1331}}), {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|qid=Q382234|1=Kusunoki Masashige}} donated a stone lantern to this shrine. In {{ill|lt=Tensh?|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1575|Q6688|qid=Q6688|1=1575}}), it received a {{ill|lt=shuinj?|Q11093673|qid=Q11093673|1=shuinjō}} from {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}} confirming its lands. In {{ill|lt=Keich?|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|qid=Q6727|1=1602}}), {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|qid=Q313641|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}} rebuilt the shrine buildings with {{ill|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|Q1882775|qid=Q1882775|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}} as magistrate. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#Various shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=g?sha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and in {{ill|lt=1894|Q7843|qid=Q7843|1=1894}} (Meiji 27) it was promoted to fusha. In {{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|qid=Q18610|1=1946}} ({{ill|lt=Sh?wa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 21), the spring grand festival, previously held annually on February 4, was moved to April 5, and that schedule continues today. === Precinct === * Honden ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|qid=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Keich?|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|qid=Q6727|1=1602}}) by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|qid=Q313641|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}} with {{ill|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|Q1882775|qid=Q1882775|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}} as magistrate. * Lantern - Donated by {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|qid=Q382234|1=Kusunoki Masashige}} in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genk?|Q1194556|qid=Q1194556|1=Genkō (1331–1334)}} ({{ill|lt=1331|Q6000|qid=Q6000|1=1331}}). * Haiden (Izumiotsu City designated tangible cultural property) - Rebuilt in the Keich? era ({{ill|lt=1596|Q6785|qid=Q6785|1=1596}} - {{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|qid=Q6784|1=1615}}) by Toyotomi Hideyori with Katagiri Katsumoto as magistrate. Two torii (Izumiotsu City designated tangible cultural properties) stand in front of the haiden; one was prepared for each of the two married deities enshrined in the honden. * Shrine office * Distant worship site * Sacred {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|qid=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} - Protected tree of Izumiotsu City. File:Izumianashi-jinja, keidai.jpg|Precinct File:Izumianashi-jinja, torii-2.jpg|Torii === Auxiliary shrines === * Sumiyoshi-sha (Important Cultural Property) - Built in {{ill|lt=Bun'ei|Q1010103|qid=Q1010103|1=Bun'ei}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1273|Q5526|qid=Q5526|1=1273}}). It is the oldest honden architecture in Osaka Prefecture. * Anashi Tenmang? (g?shi-den; Izumiotsu Furusato Cultural Heritage) - Relocated in {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|qid=Q2056|1=1908}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 41) from the honden of Sugawara Shrine in Itahara Village. * Kasuga-sha (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in Keich? 7 (1602) by Toyotomi Hideyori with Katagiri Katsumoto as magistrate. * ?kuninushi-sha - Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=?kuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}-no-Mikoto, {{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|qid=Q11059454|qid=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} * Kusunoki-sha - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|qid=Q382234|1=Kusunoki Masashige}} * Hy?zu-sha - Enshrined deity: Yachihoko-no-Kami File:Izumianashi-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Auxiliary shrine in the precinct === Festivals === * New Year's Day Festival (January 1) * Ebisu Festival (January 10) * Yakuyoke Prayer Festival (February 3) * Spring Grand Festival (April 5) * Nagoshi no ?harae (June 30) * Summer Shrine Festival (July 15) * Autumn Grand Festival (day before {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Takuhata Festival (November 1) * Winter Shrine Festival (December 15) === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (national designation) === * Izumianashi Shrine Honden (including 5 munafuda plaques) (building) - Built in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, Keich? 7 (1602). Designated on February 18, {{ill|lt=1949|Q5188|qid=Q5188|1=1949}} (Sh?wa 24){{?????????????|102|2158|???????}}. * Izumianashi Shrine Sessha Kasuga Shrine Honden (including 1 munafuda plaque) (building) - Built in the Edo period, around Keich? 7 (1602). Designated on February 18, 1949 (Sh?wa 24){{?????????????|102|2159|?????????????}}. * Izumianashi Shrine Sessha Sumiyoshi Shrine Honden (including 2 munafuda plaques) (building) - Built in the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, Bun'ei 10 (1273). Designated on February 18, 1949 (Sh?wa 24){{?????????????|102|2160|?????????????}}. * 80 wooden deity statues (sculpture) - On August 1, {{ill|lt=1899|Q7851|qid=Q7851|1=1899}} (Meiji 32), 8 statues in 4 groups were designatedSeated wooden statue of Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto, seated wooden statue of Takuhatachiji-hime-no-Mikoto, seated wooden statue of a male deity, and 5 seated wooden statues of female deities.. On August 17, {{ill|lt=2016|Q25245|qid=Q25245|1=2016}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 28), those 4 previously designated Important Cultural Properties were consolidated into 1 designation, and 72 previously undesignated deity statues were added, resulting in designation under the title "80 wooden deity statues"{{?????????????|201|3757|????}}Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 118, August 17, Heisei 28[https://web.archive.org/web/20160321192432/http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/hodo/index.php?site=fumin&pageId=23369 On designation of Important Cultural Properties (Fine Arts and Crafts)] (Osaka Prefecture website).. === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Izumianashi Shrine Wooden Group of Male and Female Deity Statues, 3 statues (sculpture) - Works from the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. Designated on February 20, {{ill|lt=1970|Q2474|qid=Q2474|1=1970}} (Sh?wa 45){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/032izumiohtuR1.pdf List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture > Izumiotsu City]}} (Osaka Prefecture website).. ** Izumianashi Shrine Drum (craft) - Designated on February 20, 1970 (Sh?wa 45). === Izumiotsu City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Izumianashi Shrine Haiden (including 2 stone torii) (building) - Built in the Keich? era (1596 - 1615). Designated on March 23, {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|qid=Q1985|1=2000}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 12). * Natural monuments ** Group of 12 giant camphor trees at Izumianashi Shrine - Designated on March 23, {{ill|lt=1999|Q2091|qid=Q2091|1=1999}} (Heisei 11). === Izumiotsu Furusato Cultural Heritage === * Tangible cultural property ** Izumianashi Shrine G?shi-den (building) - Certified on January 23, {{ill|lt=2013|Q1998|qid=Q1998|1=2013}} (Heisei 25). === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === See also === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Anashi Yakushid?|1=UNKNOWN}} - Formerly this shrine's {{ill|lt=jing?-ji|Q1074971|qid=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. === External links === {{Commonscat|Izumianashi-jinja}} * [http://izumi.anashi-jinja.com Izumianashi Shrine] - Official site * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=40201 Izumianashi Shrine (two seats)] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{????????}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Izumianashi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Izumianashi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:泉大津市の建築物]] [[Category:泉大津市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Architecture of Kamakura period]] [[Category:Architecture of Azuchi-Momoyama period]] [[Category:Izumi Province]] [[Category:Ni-no-Miya]] [[Category:Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]]
